params.text:
Continence products are one part of care

Continence products — such as pads, pants and sometimes other aids — can help people stay comfortable, confident and active. They are a valuable support, but they are only ever one part of good continence care, used within a plan that also promotes independence. Reaching for a pad should never replace helping someone get to the toilet.

  • Right product: the type and size are decided by assessment, not guesswork.
  • Used with dignity: support privately, calmly and respectfully.
  • Change promptly: check and change products as needed to protect skin.

Using products respectfully

The type of product a person uses is decided through their continence assessment, so it suits their needs, is the right size and fit, and does not limit their independence more than necessary. When you support someone with a product, do so with the same care and privacy you would want yourself: explain what you are doing, keep the person covered, work gently, and never make comments that could cause embarrassment.

Check products regularly and change them promptly when needed, because a wet or soiled pad left in place quickly makes skin sore and undermines dignity. Handle used products hygienically and dispose of them correctly. If a product is leaking, uncomfortable, or no longer seems right for the person, report it so the plan can be reviewed — do not simply switch to a different product yourself.

params.question: How should continence products be used within a person's care?

answers.text: As one part of a plan that still promotes independence, with the right product used privately and changed promptly.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. Products support care but never replace toileting support, and must be used with dignity and good skin care.

answers.text: As a replacement for helping the person get to the toilet.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Products should support, not replace, toileting support and independence.

answers.text: By choosing whatever pad is nearest, regardless of fit or assessment.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: The right product is decided by assessment — the wrong size or type can leak and harm skin.

answers.text: By leaving them in place as long as possible to save changes.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Leaving a wet or soiled product in place makes skin sore and removes dignity.
